growth_records.medium: Phosphate-buffered minimal medium with TRIS as sole carbon and nitrogen source
growth_records.cite: Holert et al. Bacteria use a catabolic patchwork pathway of apparently recent origin for degradation of the synthetic buffer compound TRIS. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrad023. [PMC10848231]
growth_records.snippet: The isolate had an exponential growth rate of 0.36 ± 0.09 h−1 and a doubling time of 0.44 ± 0.09 h (n = 6).
growth_records.notes: Both growth_rate_per_h (0.36 +/- 0.09 h-1) and doubling_time_h (0.44 +/- 0.09 h, n=6) are directly reported verbatim in the text for strain Teo1 grown on 10 mM TRIS as sole C/N source, 30C, pH 7.0, 200 rpm shaking (batch, phosphate-buffered minimal medium). Note: as stated in the paper these two values are numerically inconsistent with each other (ln2/0.36 h-1 would imply a doubling time of ~1.93 h, not 0.44 h); both are reproduced here exactly as written in the source text without correction. Trace elements component of medium referenced to an external protocol [ref 17] and not quantified in the accessible text; oxygenation not explicitly labeled 'aerobic' in text (200 rpm shaking culture). [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]
